title: Gravity Mod
description: Add realistic physics, gravity-defying staves, tactical grenades, and chaos hammers to your survival world!
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/CGjeO2v4Oss" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>
Gravity Mod v2.0 - The Anomaly Update

Gravity Mod brings actual physics to Minecraft 1.20.1. Blocks placed by players or hit with special tools now obey gravity—break the support, and everything above comes crashing down.

🔥 New in v2.0: The Anomaly Generator 🔥
The biggest addition to the mod. It’s a machine that completely disables gravity in a 15-block radius.

How it works: Place it, open the custom UI, and turn the power on. Any unsupported blocks in its range will freeze in mid-air instead of falling. Perfect for building sky bases or floating islands. You can also toggle a particle border to see the exact range.

The catch: If you turn the generator off or break it, gravity instantly kicks back in. Every single block that was floating will collapse at the exact same time.

Tools & Items:

Chaos Hammer: Whack a block to update its physics. It and everything attached above it will fall.

Gravity Stick: Freeze a single block in the air so it doesn't fall. Essential for building roofs.

Levitation Staff: Launch a target block high into the sky.

Gravity Grenade: Creates a kinetic shockwave that launches nearby blocks around.

Super Slime Block: Acts as a bounce pad for falling blocks.

Loot Magnet: Sucks up all dropped items in a wide radius.

Hard Hat: Wear this so you don't get crushed by your own collapsing buildings.

Gravity Apple: Gives you a quick Levitation effect.

Survival Ready: Everything is craftable and balanced for regular playthroughs. Basic tools require early-game materials (copper, clay, feathers), while the Anomaly Generator requires gold and coal.

Requires Minecraft Forge 1.20.1.
